_meta.purpose: Image-edit pass — take the L9 'Knight-Sergeant' composited keeper and produce the L9.5 'Royal-Grade Bridge' variant, an intermediate step between L9 and L10.
history: v1 (2026-05-06): initial author — Royal-Grade Bridge = 4 visible adds (gold spike caps at the tips of each L9 helm horn, gold cross emblem inlaid on the shield face centered over the boss, gold knuckle-plates replacing dark-iron knuckle-rivets on each gauntlet, fine gold edge piping running around the perimeter of the cuirass and each tasset). All adds expand the gold accents established at L9 — no new shapes introduced. Within-tier chained input strategy. Bridge level designed to absorb the visual jump to L10 (which adds the tall central crest, ornate gold flanged mace, sculpted lion's-head pauldron motif, and ruby-gem boss in a single step).

v2 (2026-05-07): revised per AD FeedbackV0 — REMOVED gold gauntlet knuckle-plates per AD detail-economy ('per-knuckle gold plates' on the canonical 'first cut' list — surface ornament with low thumbnail readability at gauntlet scale). REMOVED fine gold edge piping on the cuirass + tassets per AD detail-economy ('per-edge gold piping is the canonical first-cut item'). NEW VOLUME-bump add: HORNS GROW TALLER and more sharply curved (volume upgrade replaces the deprecated knuckle-plate + edge-piping passes; sets up the L10 crested helm). Carry-overs: gold spike caps on horn tips + gold cross emblem on shield face. Updated CRITICAL PRESERVATION: L9 has NO pauldron/tasset filigree (removed at L9), tassets at L9 have pronounced outward flare (volume), L8 first-gold = helm trim band only.
v3 (2026-05-07): pipeline update — drop L1_Base scale anchor from image_urls and prompt body. Single-image input (prior-level composited keeper only) per updated pipeline + docs.

v6 (2026-05-07): scrub model-invisible context from prompt body — removed L<n> level-tag references (model is stateless and cannot resolve our level naming), authoring-decision parentheticals (LOCKED/REVISED/REMOVED dates, per AD FeedbackV0, etc.), and cross-level range refs. All persistent decisions remain in _meta.history + the progression plan + CLAUDE.md. See Archer/CLAUDE.md § Part 3 locked decision #27.
_meta.tier: Royal — Bridge (between L9 Knight-Sergeant and L10 Royal Champion)

adds.title: GOLD POINTED SPIKE CAPS AT EACH HORN TIP
adds.summary: replace the steel pointed tips of each L9 helm horn with BUTTERY POLISHED GOLD spike caps.

adds.title: GOLD CROSS EMBLEM INLAID ON THE SHIELD FACE CENTERED OVER THE BOSS
adds.summary: add a four-armed cross emblem inlaid on the steel face of the round shield, centered over the existing sun-burst boss.

adds.title: HORNS GROW TALLER AND MORE SHARPLY CURVED
adds.summary: extend each existing helm horn's height from approximately 6 inches at L9 to approximately 8-9 inches at L9.5, and increase the outward+back sweep so the horns curve more sharply away from the helm body before tapering to their pointed gold-capped tips.

image_urls: <<UPLOAD: UnitProgression/Infantry/out/v1/L9/composite/infantry_L9_v3_composited.png>> (content reference — apply the upgrades onto this character)
prompt: CRITICAL PRESERVATION VERIFICATION (READ FIRST) — The input image is the canonical content reference. It already shows the 'Knight-Sergeant' kit accumulated on top of the existing base. You MUST preserve every prior feature shown in the input image: (a) the STEEL SALLET HELM with its forward-projecting brim, dome, brim rivets, and gold band wrapping the helm at the brim line; (b) the CLOSED VISOR with horizontal eye slit and breathing perforations at mouth level — the FACE remains FULLY CONCEALED at this level (no skin / hair / eyes / nose / mustache / beard visible above the bevor); (c) the MODEST UPWARD-CURVING STEEL HORNS rising from the helm temples (~6 inches tall at this level, swept slightly outward and back) — at these horns GROW TALLER and more sharply curved per Edit Goal item 3 AND gain gold pointed spike caps at their tips per Edit Goal item 1; (d) the FULL STEEL CUIRASS — RAISED MEDIAL RIDGE plus ONE simple horizontal seam at the bottom-of-sternum line (NOT 3 articulated lames; engraved scrollwork was REMOVED at — the cuirass has NO surface engraving); preserved at with NO gold edge piping added (per-edge gold piping was REMOVED 2026-05-07 'per-edge gold piping is the canonical first-cut item'); (e) the WIDE WARM-BROWN LEATHER WAIST BELT with square dark-iron buckle visible between the tassets at the front; (f) the LONGER 2-LAME STEEL TASSETS extending to mid-thigh — exactly 2 WIDER lames per tasset (NOT 3 narrow lames) with the existing PRONOUNCED OUTWARD FLARE at the lower edge — preserved at this level; NO gold filigree on the tassets (filigree REMOVED at), NO gold edge piping around the tasset perimeters (per-edge piping REMOVED at); (g) the 3-LAME STEEL PAULDRONS — preserved at in shape; NO gold rivet heads at the pauldron seam (the existing first-gold beat moved to a clear gold helm trim band; pauldron-seam rivets are NOT gold), NO gold filigree on the upper face of each pauldron (filigree REMOVED); (h) the STEEL VAMBRACES extending continuously into STEEL GAUNTLETS at the wrists — preserved at this level; the existing dark-iron knuckle-rivets on each gauntlet remain dark iron (NO gold knuckle-plates — gauntlet gold knuckle-plates were REMOVED 2026-05-07 'per-knuckle gold plates on gauntlets' — surface ornament with low thumbnail readability); (i) the SOLID FLANGED STEEL MACE HEAD with 6 vertical flanges held in the gauntleted anatomical-RIGHT hand — preserved at this level (mace head remains all-steel; gold gilt-flanged ornate mace arrives at this level); (j) the WOODEN MACE HAFT with leather grip cord on upper third and iron reinforcement strip running its length; (k) the LARGER STEEL-FACED ROUND SHIELD with the embossed sun-burst boss (raised central dome with 8 short triangular rays in cool silver-gray steel), the gold ring around the boss base, the GOLD RIM BAND with ~16 gold rivet heads, the four iron cross strap-bands radiating from the boss in dark cool-gray iron — the new step ADDS a gold cross emblem inlay on the shield face per Edit Goal item 2 but does NOT change the rim, the boss, or the strap-bands; (l) the STEEL SABATONS over warm-brown leather boots; (m) the LIVERY SKIRT BAND visible through the narrow ~2-inch front gap between the two tassets — the red+mustard split tunic skirt band remains visible at this level; (n) the BROWN LEATHER HIP POUCH on anatomical-RIGHT hip and the BROWN LEATHER KNIFE SHEATH with bone-handle knife on anatomical-LEFT hip; (o) the MAIL COIF back-of-neck and shoulder drape behind/below the helm. DO NOT revert any of these features. EDIT GOAL — Apply THREE visible additive upgrades to the infantryman shown in the input image, marking the royal-grade bridge between Knight-Sergeant and Royal Champion. The first two adds expand the existing gold accents (gold horn-tip caps, gold cross emblem on shield); the third is a SILHOUETTE / VOLUME bump (horns grow taller and more sharply curved) replacing the deprecated gauntlet knuckle-plate + cuirass/tassets edge-piping passes 'rely on shapes/silhouettes, not sculpted details/filigree' — per-knuckle gold plates and per-edge gold piping are on the canonical first-cut list. Cleaner gold map; the volume bump preps the silhouette for the existing crested helm. CRITICAL gold rendering note: every gold element MUST render as BUTTERY POLISHED YELLOW GOLD METAL with a clearly distinct warm-yellow hue contrasting strongly against the cool silver-gray steel surfaces. The gold should be SATURATED and IMMEDIATELY READABLE as gold at thumbnail scale — NOT brass, NOT bronze, NOT yellow paint, NOT mustard tint, NOT a warm-tinted highlight on steel. Render gold with: (i) a saturated buttery-yellow base tone (warm yellow-orange, like polished 18-karat gold, distinctly more yellow than the surrounding cool gray steel); (ii) a soft top-down highlight that reads almost white-yellow; (iii) a deeper warm shadow on the underside that reads honey-orange. (1) GOLD POINTED SPIKE CAPS AT EACH HORN TIP — replace the steel pointed tips of each helm horn with BUTTERY POLISHED GOLD spike caps. Each spike cap is a small gold cone approximately 1.5 inches tall, replacing roughly the upper third of each horn with gold while the lower two thirds of each horn remain cool silver-gray steel. The transition from steel to gold is a clean horizontal line where the cap meets the horn body. The gold caps must be IMMEDIATELY READABLE as gold metal at thumbnail scale, distinctly contrasting with the steel horn body below. (2) GOLD CROSS EMBLEM INLAID ON THE SHIELD FACE CENTERED OVER THE BOSS — add a four-armed cross emblem inlaid on the steel face of the round shield, centered over the existing sun-burst boss. The cross has equal-length arms (about 4 inches arm length each), each arm approximately 1.5 inches wide, all rendered in BUTTERY POLISHED GOLD as a flat inlay flush with the steel shield face (NOT raised relief at this level — the cross is inlaid, lying flat in the steel surface). The cross arms extend OUT FROM the central boss area: the cross's center sits behind/around the boss dome (the boss dome and sun-burst rays remain cool silver-gray and visible at the cross's center where the arms intersect); the four arms radiate outward over the steel shield face but stop SHORT of the iron cross strap-bands and the gold rim band. The new gold cross sits visually OVER the steel face but UNDER the existing iron cross strap-bands and the central boss. The cross emblem must read clearly as a four-armed cross at thumbnail scale. (3) HORNS GROW TALLER AND MORE SHARPLY CURVED (VOLUME BUMP per silhouette-mightiness rule, replacing the deprecated gauntlet knuckle-plate + cuirass/tassets edge-piping passes) — extend each existing helm horn's height from approximately 6 inches at to approximately 8-9 inches at this level, and increase the outward+back sweep so the horns curve more sharply away from the helm body before tapering to their pointed gold-capped tips. The horn bodies remain cool silver-gray polished steel; the gold pointed spike caps from Edit Goal item 1 sit at the new taller tips (the gold caps grow proportionally to remain at the upper third of each taller horn). The helm-plus-horns silhouette now reads as a clearly RISING-RANK volume bump — the helm silhouette extends visibly UPWARD from to, preparing for the existing central gold crest. NO gold knuckle-plates are added on the gauntlets at this level (per-knuckle gold plates were REMOVED 2026-05-07 — surface ornament with low thumbnail readability at gauntlet scale); the existing dark-iron knuckle-rivets remain dark iron. NO gold edge piping is added on the cuirass or tassets at this level (per-edge gold piping was REMOVED 2026-05-07 — per-edge piping is the canonical first-cut item); cuirass + tassets remain plain steel with no perimeter gold line. ===== PRESERVE FROM THE INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY ===== every other element of the input image must remain identical in shape, color, position, and proportion: the helm dome, brim, brim rivets, gold helm circumference band; the closed visor with horizontal eye slit and breathing perforations; the steel horn bodies (now taller per Edit Goal item 3; tips become gold per Edit Goal item 1); the cuirass with its raised medial ridge and ONE simple horizontal seam (NO 3-lame articulation, NO engraved scrollwork — both removed at this level); the 3-lame pauldrons with NO gold rivets at the seam (pauldron-seam gold was moved to a clear gold helm trim band at this level) and NO gold filigree on the upper face (filigree removed at); the steel vambraces with dark-iron knuckle-rivets at the gauntlet knuckles (NO gold knuckle-plates 2026-05-07); the wide leather belt with iron buckle; the brown leather hip pouch on anatomical-RIGHT hip — the ONLY belt accessory, NO knife/dagger sheath; the longer 2-lame steel tassets with pronounced outward flare at the lower edge (carried over from the prior level), NO gold filigree, NO gold edge piping; the LIVERY skirt band visible through the front tasset gap; the dark blue trousers visible at the calf; the steel sabatons over warm-brown leather boots; the leather grip cord on the upper haft; the iron reinforcement strip on the wooden haft; the wooden mace haft (haft remains wood; head is the existing flanged steel head); the flanged steel mace head with 6 vertical flanges (steel only — gold gilt-flanged mace arrives at this level); the larger steel-faced round shield with the central steel boss + sun-burst rays + gold ring around boss base + four iron cross strap-bands + gold rim band with ~16 gold rivet heads; the mail coif back-of-neck and shoulder drape behind/below the helm. ===== POSE (must match the input image exactly) ===== Three-quarter view, body angled approximately fifteen to twenty-five degrees to the viewer's left, weight planted evenly on both feet with slight forward lean. THE MACE ARM (anatomical-RIGHT, viewer-LEFT) extended forward and slightly DOWNWARD, gauntleted hand grips the upper haft. THE SHIELD ARM (anatomical-LEFT, viewer-RIGHT) bent at elbow, round shield up at upper-chest / shoulder height. Calm guard stance. ===== STYLE / OUTLINE ===== Supercell / Kingshot stylized mobile game art, hand-painted digital illustration with soft painted shading and visible brushwork, saturated rich colors, warm shadow tones never gray, soft painted highlights on metal. Clean thin black silhouette outline in deep warm brown-black with even weight. Interior linework remains soft and value-driven, never hard black. ===== FRAMING / SCALE ===== 1024x1024 square canvas. Match the framing of the input image as closely as possible (no new vertical extensions at this level). DO NOT zoom in further, DO NOT crop the horns at the top, DO NOT shift the figure downward, DO NOT shrink the figure. Clean white background — no scenery, no ground plane, minimal contact shadow, 1:1 square aspect ratio. ===== NEGATIVE — DO NOT ===== DO NOT open the visor at this level — visor remains FULLY CLOSED, face FULLY CONCEALED; DO NOT show any skin, hair, eyes (other than the slit), nose, mustache, or beard above the bevor; DO NOT add a tall central crest between the horns at this level — the central crest arrives at this level; DO NOT shrink the horns at this level — they GROW TALLER (~8-9 inches up from ~6 inches) and more sharply curved per the new volume-bump add (the gauntlet knuckle-plate + cuirass/tassets edge-piping passes were REMOVED 2026-05-07; DO NOT make the horn bodies fully gold — only the upper third (the cap) is gold; the lower two-thirds remain cool silver-gray steel; DO NOT add gold knuckle-plates on the gauntlets at this level — per-knuckle gold plates were REMOVED ('per-knuckle gold plates on gauntlets' is on the canonical first-cut list); the dark-iron knuckle-rivets remain dark iron; DO NOT add gold edge piping / gold perimeter line / gold trim lines on the cuirass or tassets at this level — per-edge gold piping was REMOVED ('per-edge gold piping is the canonical first-cut item'); cuirass + tassets remain plain steel with no perimeter gold line; DO NOT add gold filigree / scrollwork on the pauldrons or tassets at this level — filigree was REMOVED at this level; DO NOT make the gold cross emblem on the shield raised relief — at the cross is inlaid flush with the steel face (raised gold cross arrives at this level); DO NOT make the cross arms extend onto the iron cross strap-bands or onto the gold rim band — the gold cross stays inside the steel face area between the boss (center) and the strap-bands (outer); DO NOT change the iron cross strap-bands to gold — they remain dark cool-gray iron at this level; DO NOT change the central boss dome to gold — the boss DOME remains cool silver-gray steel (only the gold ring around the boss base from remains; no full gold boss dome at this level); DO NOT add a ruby gem at the boss center — that arrives at this level; DO NOT make the gauntlet finger-plates gold — only the knuckle-plates (one per knuckle, 4 per hand) are gold; the articulated finger-plates remain steel; DO NOT remove the steel articulated finger-plates; DO NOT make the cuirass medial ridge gold — the tonal engraving remains; only the perimeter piping is gold; DO NOT extend the gold piping across the body of the cuirass — only the outline edge; DO NOT make the gold dim, brass-toned, bronze-toned, or yellow-painted — gold MUST render as buttery polished saturated YELLOW GOLD METAL; DO NOT make the mace head gold or partially gold — gold gilt-flanged mace arrives at this level; DO NOT replace the wooden mace haft with metal; DO NOT change the round shield to any non-round shape; DO NOT shrink the livery skirt band to nothing — it remains visible; DO NOT change the trouser color or boot color (boots remain warm-brown leather where the sabatons do not cover); DO NOT change the chibi proportions; DO NOT swap the hand holding the mace; DO NOT swap the hand holding the shield; DO NOT change the camera angle, framing, or composition; DO NOT crop the figure (especially do not crop the horns at the top); DO NOT change the background, add scenery, ground plane, or cast shadows; DO NOT make the background transparent, checkerboard-patterned, or non-white; DO NOT drift the art style toward anime, photorealism, fully painterly, flat shading, cell shading, or pixel art; DO NOT add a glow, rim light, blue rim light, cyan rim light, or any new light source; DO NOT change the aspect ratio from 1:1 square.
